name: Alpha Arbutin 2% + HA
what: A water-light serum pairing alpha arbutin with hyaluronic acid.
why: Arbutin slows the enzyme that makes excess pigment, so marks fade without irritating your skin the way stronger actives can.
order: After cleansing and toning, before moisturizer — plays nicely with almost everything.

name: Discoloration Correcting Serum
what: A cult-favorite serum combining tranexamic acid with niacinamide.
why: The duo targets stubborn post-acne marks and melasma-type patches from two angles at once — for the price of a lunch.
order: Morning or night after cleansing, before heavier layers.

name: Tranexamic Topical Acid 5%
what: A higher-strength tranexamic treatment with kojic acid and niacinamide.
why: Tranexamic acid is one of the best-studied ingredients for discoloration that hasn't budged with gentler options.
order: After watery serums, before moisturizer; start 3 nights a week.

name: Vitamin C Serum
what: A stabilized vitamin C serum with vitamin E and hyaluronic acid.
why: Vitamin C brightens overall tone and helps defend against the daily damage that creates new spots.
order: Morning, after cleansing — then SPF locks in the benefit.

name: Mela B3 Dark Spot Serum
what: A dermatologist-developed serum built around the newer Melasyl molecule.
why: One of the most exciting recent launches for discoloration — strong results in trials, gentle enough for daily use.
order: Once daily after cleansing, before moisturizer.

name: Even Tone Cleansing Bar
what: A cleansing bar with kojic acid, azelaic acid and niacinamide, made for melanin-rich skin.
why: Treats uneven tone while you wash, so you get active benefits even on lazy nights.
order: Use as your cleanser a few nights a week; leave on 1–2 minutes as a mini mask.

name: Kojic Acid Soap
what: The classic kojic acid soap for body discoloration.
why: An affordable way to work on dark marks on arms, legs, underarms and bikini area in the shower.
order: In the shower a few times a week; lather, leave briefly, rinse well.
caution: Can be drying — leave on under a minute at first and moisturize after.

name: Skin Perfecting 2% BHA Liquid
what: A leave-on salicylic acid exfoliant.
why: BHA is oil-soluble, so it clears inside the pore — the single most recommended product for blackheads and congestion.
order: After cleansing, 2–3 nights a week to start; don't layer with other strong acids the same night.
caution: Increases sun sensitivity — SPF every morning.

name: BHA Blackhead Power Liquid
what: A mild betaine-salicylate exfoliant.
why: A softer entry point to BHA if stronger versions sting — still clears congestion over a few weeks.
order: After cleansing on alternate nights; build up slowly.
caution: Increases sun sensitivity — SPF every morning.

name: Differin Gel 0.1%
what: Adapalene, a retinoid formerly prescription-only, now over the counter.
why: The most effective non-prescription step for persistent acne — it normalizes how pores shed so breakouts stop forming.
order: Pea-sized amount at night on dry skin; start 2–3 nights a week.
caution: Expect a 4–6 week adjustment period; not for use while pregnant.

name: Acne Foaming Cream Cleanser
what: A creamy benzoyl peroxide wash with ceramides.
why: BPO kills acne bacteria on contact; the rinse-off format gets the benefit with far less dryness than leave-ons.
order: Use as your cleanser on breakout-prone days; keep it to once daily.
caution: Bleaches towels and pillowcases — use white ones.

name: PanOxyl 10% Acne Wash
what: The strongest OTC benzoyl peroxide wash.
why: The go-to for chest and back breakouts — lather, let it sit 60 seconds, rinse.
order: In the shower a few times a week; moisturize after.
caution: Bleaches fabric; 10% can be drying on the face — the 4% version is gentler there.

name: Mighty Patch Original
what: Hydrocolloid stickers that flatten whiteheads overnight.
why: They absorb the gunk, protect the spot from picking, and the mark heals faster because you left it alone.
order: On clean, dry skin as the last step at night — skincare goes around, not under.

name: Niacinamide 10% + Zinc 1%
what: A high-strength niacinamide serum with zinc.
why: Calms breakouts, regulates oil, fades marks and strengthens your barrier — the best value-per-dollar serum in skincare.
order: Morning or night after cleansing; layers under everything.

name: Effaclar Duo Dual Action
tag: Treats the spot and the mark
what: A benzoyl peroxide treatment that also targets the mark left behind.
why: Most acne treatments ignore the dark spot that follows the pimple — this one works on both.
order: Thin layer on breakout-prone areas after cleansing.
caution: Contains benzoyl peroxide — can bleach fabric.

name: Lactic Acid 10% + HA
what: A lactic acid exfoliating serum buffered with hyaluronic acid.
why: Lactic acid smooths and brightens with less sting than glycolic — a lovely first AHA.
order: At night, 2–3 times a week; never the same night as retinoids.
caution: Increases sun sensitivity — SPF every morning.

name: Glycolic Acid 7% Toner
what: A daily-ish glycolic exfoliating toner.
why: Famous for face glow and doubles beautifully for body — underarms, KP-prone arms, ingrown-prone areas.
order: On a cotton pad after cleansing at night, 2–3 times a week.
caution: Increases sun sensitivity — SPF every morning. Skip on freshly shaved skin.

name: KP Bump Eraser Body Scrub
what: A physical + chemical scrub made for keratosis pilaris ('strawberry skin').
why: The AHA dissolves the keratin plugs while the pumice polishes — visibly smoother arms and legs in weeks.
order: In the shower 2–3 times a week on damp skin, then moisturize.

name: Daily Moisturizing Lotion
what: A body lotion that exfoliates and moisturizes in one step.
why: The dermatologist classic for rough, bumpy body skin — it works while feeling like plain lotion.
order: Daily after showering; not right after shaving.

name: AHA/BHA Clarifying Toner
what: A very mild dual-acid toner.
why: Acid benefits at training-wheels strength — great for keeping texture smooth between stronger treatment nights.
order: After cleansing, before serums; gentle enough for near-daily use.

name: Alpha Beta Universal Daily Peel
what: Pre-dosed two-step peel pads with a blend of five acids.
why: The closest thing to a professional peel at home — the splurge that makes skin look polished for events.
order: Step 1, wait 2 minutes, step 2; start with 2–3 times a week.
caution: Increases sun sensitivity — SPF every morning.

name: Advanced Snail 96 Mucin Essence
what: A 96% snail secretion filtrate essence.
why: Deeply hydrating and repair-focused — beloved for calming post-acne skin and giving that dewy 'glass skin' finish.
order: After toning, before heavier serums and creams.

name: Hydro Boost Water Gel
what: A hyaluronic acid gel-cream.
why: Real hydration with zero heaviness — the moisturizer for skin that hates feeling like it's wearing moisturizer.
order: Last skincare step at night, or before SPF in the morning.

name: Moisturizing Cream
what: The ceramide-rich classic for face and body.
why: Restores the skin barrier that actives chip away at — the safety net every routine with acids or retinoids needs.
order: Face: last step. Body: after every shower.

name: Cicaplast Baume B5+
what: A panthenol repair balm.
why: When your skin is angry from too many actives, this is the reset button — barrier repair in a tube.
order: As your moisturizer (or over it) on recovery nights; skip actives those nights.

name: Hyaluronic Acid 2% + B5
what: A multi-weight hyaluronic acid serum.
why: Pulls water into the skin so everything layered on top works on plump, hydrated skin instead of thirsty skin.
order: On damp skin right after cleansing, then seal with moisturizer.

name: Water Sleeping Mask
what: A leave-on overnight hydrating mask.
why: You wake up looking like you drank three liters of water and got nine hours of sleep, even when neither is true.
order: As the last step at night, 2–3 times a week instead of (or over) moisturizer.

name: Cicapair Tiger Grass Cream
what: A centella-based color-correcting treatment.
why: The green tint neutralizes redness instantly while centella calms it for real over time.
order: After serums, in place of or before moisturizer.

name: Calm + Restore Oat Gel
what: A prebiotic oat gel moisturizer.
why: Oat is one of the best-proven soothers in skincare — drugstore price, derm-office reputation.
order: Morning and night as your moisturizer on sensitive days.

name: Thermal Spring Water Spray
what: A mineral spring water mist.
why: Instant relief for hot, irritated or freshly-treated skin — keep one in the fridge.
order: Anytime skin feels reactive; pat in, don't let it evaporate off.

name: Toleriane Hydrating Cleanser
what: A creamy, non-foaming ceramide cleanser.
why: Cleans without stripping — the right first step when the rest of your routine has strong actives.
order: Morning and night as your first step.

name: Granactive Retinoid 2% Emulsion
what: A next-generation retinoid in a milky emulsion.
why: Retinoid results — smoother texture, fewer fine lines, clearer pores — with far less irritation than retinol.
order: At night after water-based serums; start twice a week.
caution: Not for use while pregnant; SPF every morning.

name: Retinol Correxion Deep Wrinkle Night Cream
what: A time-tested retinol night cream.
why: Decades of clinical receipts for softening lines — an easy one-step way to add retinol without building a layering routine.
order: At night in place of moisturizer, 3 nights a week to start.
caution: Not for use while pregnant; SPF every morning.

name: Resurfacing Retinol Serum
what: An encapsulated retinol serum with ceramides and licorice root.
why: Made specifically for post-acne texture and marks — gentle enough to be many people's first retinol.
order: At night after cleansing; start 2–3 nights a week.
caution: Not for use while pregnant; SPF every morning.

name: UV Clear SPF 46
what: A niacinamide-infused mineral-hybrid sunscreen for acne-prone skin.
why: The sunscreen dermatologists actually wear — no breakouts, no white cast drama, and it calms while it protects.
order: Every morning, last step, ideally two finger-lengths' worth.

name: Relief Sun: Rice + Probiotics SPF 50+
what: A Korean chemical sunscreen that feels like skincare.
why: If you skip SPF because it feels like SPF, this is the fix — every spot-fading product works better with daily sun protection.
order: Every morning as the final step; reapply if you're out long.

name: Unseen Sunscreen SPF 40
what: A totally clear, velvety gel sunscreen.
why: Invisible on every skin tone and doubles as a makeup primer — removes the last excuse for skipping SPF.
order: Every morning, last skincare step / first makeup step.

name: Anthelios Melt-in Milk SPF 60
what: A high-protection lotion sunscreen for face and body.
why: One bottle covers beach days, body brightening projects, and daily wear — sun exposure is what keeps dark marks alive.
order: Every morning; generously on any exposed body areas you're treating.

name: The Glow Getter Body Wash
what: A glycolic + salicylic body wash.
why: Gently resurfaces body skin while you shower — great support for bacne, bumps and uneven tone on the body.
order: A few showers a week; let it sit a minute before rinsing.

name: Thigh Rescue Anti-Chafe Stick
what: A glide-on stick that prevents thigh chafing.
why: Chafing causes the friction darkness and rough patches many people try to fade — preventing it beats treating it.
order: Swipe on before dresses, workouts or hot days.

name: Azelaic Acid Suspension 10%
what: A cream-gel azelaic acid treatment.
why: Underrated triple threat: calms redness and breakouts, fades marks, and is one of the few actives considered safe in pregnancy.
order: Morning or night after serums; can sit alongside most actives.
